644:   # Activates/desactivates email deliveries during +block+
645:   def self.with_deliveries(enabled = true, &)
646:     was_enabled = ActionMailer::Base.perform_deliveries
647:     ActionMailer::Base.perform_deliveries = !!enabled
648:     yield
649:   ensure
650:     ActionMailer::Base.perform_deliveries = was_enabled
651:   end
652: 
653:   # Execute the given block with inline sending of emails if the default Async
654:   # queue is used for the mailer. See the Rails guide:
655:   # Using the asynchronous queue from a Rake task will generally not work because
656:   # Rake will likely end, causing the in-process thread pool to be deleted, before
657:   # any/all of the .deliver_later emails are processed
658:   def self.with_synched_deliveries(&)
659:     adapter = ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob.queue_adapter
660:     ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob.queue_adapter = ActiveJob::QueueAdapters::InlineAdapter.new
661:     yield
662:   ensure
663:     ActionMailer::MailDeliveryJob.queue_adapter = adapter
664:   end
665: 
666:   def mail(headers={}, &block)
667:     begin
668:       # Add a display name to the From field if Setting.mail_from does not
669:       # include it
670:       mail_from = Mail::Address.new(Setting.mail_from)
671:       if mail_from.display_name.blank? && mail_from.comments.blank?
672:         mail_from.display_name =
673:           @author&.logged? ? @author.name : Setting.app_title
674:       end
675:       from = mail_from.format
676: 
677:       # Construct the value of the List-Id header field
678:       from_addr = mail_from.address.to_s
679:       project_identifier = self.headers['X-Redmine-Project']&.value
680:       list_id = if project_identifier.present?
681:                   "<#{project_identifier}.#{from_addr.tr('@', '.')}>"
682:                 else
683:                   # Emails outside of a project context
684:                   "<#{from_addr.tr('@', '.')}>"
685:                 end
686:     rescue Mail::Field::IncompleteParseError
687:       # Use Setting.mail_from as it is if Mail::Address cannot parse it
688:       # (probably the emission address is not RFC compliant)
689:       from = Setting.mail_from.to_s
690:       list_id = "<#{from.tr('@', '.')}>"
691:     end
692: 
693:     headers.reverse_merge! 'X-Mailer' => 'Redmine',
694:             'X-Redmine-Host' => Setting.host_name,
695:             'X-Redmine-Site' => Setting.app_title,
696:             'X-Auto-Response-Suppress' => 'All',
697:             'Auto-Submitted' => 'auto-generated',
698:             'From' => from,
699:             'List-Id' => list_id
700: 
701:     # Replaces users with their email addresses
702:     [:to, :cc, :bcc].each do |key|
703:       if headers[key].present?
704:         headers[key] = self.class.email_addresses(headers[key])
705:       end
706:     end
707: 
708:     # Removes the author from the recipients and cc
709:     # if the author does not want to receive notifications
710:     # about what the author do
711:     if @author&.logged? && @author.pref.no_self_notified
712:       addresses = @author.mails
713:       headers[:to] -= addresses if headers[:to].is_a?(Array)
714:       headers[:cc] -= addresses if headers[:cc].is_a?(Array)
715:     end
716: 
717:     if @author&.logged?
718:       redmine_headers 'Sender' => @author.login
719:     end
720: 
721:     if @message_id_object
722:       headers[:message_id] = "<#{self.class.message_id_for(@message_id_object, @user)}>"
723:     end
724:     if @references_objects
725:       headers[:references] = @references_objects.collect {|o| "<#{self.class.references_for(o, @user)}>"}.join(' ')
726:     end
727: 
728:     if block
729:       super
730:     else
731:       super do |format|
732:         format.text
733:         format.html unless Setting.plain_text_mail?
734:       end
735:     end
736:   end
737: 
738:   def self.deliver_mail(mail)
739:     return false if mail.to.blank? && mail.cc.blank? && mail.bcc.blank?
740: 
741:     begin
742:       # Log errors when raise_delivery_errors is set to false, Rails does not
743:       mail.raise_delivery_errors = true
744:       super
745:     rescue => e
746:       if ActionMailer::Base.raise_delivery_errors
747:         raise e
748:       else
749:         Rails.logger.error "Email delivery error: #{e.message}"
750:       end
751:     end
752:   end
753: 
754:   # Returns an array of email addresses to notify by
755:   # replacing users in arg with their notified email addresses
756:   #
757:   # Example:
758:   #   Mailer.email_addresses(users)
759:   #   => ["foo@example.net", "bar@example.net"]
760:   def self.email_addresses(arg)
761:     arr = Array.wrap(arg)
762:     mails = arr.grep_v(Principal)
763:     users = arr - mails
764:     if users.any?
765:       mails += EmailAddress.
766:         where(:user_id => users.map(&:id)).
767:         where("is_default = ? OR notify = ?", true, true).
768:         pluck(:address)
769:     end
770:     mails
771:   end

775:   # Appends a Redmine header field (name is prepended with 'X-Redmine-')
776:   def redmine_headers(h)
777:     h.compact.each {|k, v| headers["X-Redmine-#{k}"] = v.to_s}
778:   end

780:   def assignee_for_header(issue)
781:     case issue.assigned_to
782:     when User
783:       issue.assigned_to.login
784:     when Group
785:       "Group (#{issue.assigned_to.name})"
786:     end
787:   end

789:   # Singleton class method is public
790:   class << self
791:     def token_for(object, user)
792:       timestamp = object.send(object.respond_to?(:created_on) ? :created_on : :updated_on)
793:       hash = [
794:         "redmine",
795:         "#{object.class.name.demodulize.underscore}-#{object.id}",
796:         timestamp.utc.strftime("%Y%m%d%H%M%S")
797:       ]
798:       hash << user.id if user
799:       host = Setting.mail_from.to_s.strip.gsub(%r{^.*@|>}, '')
800:       host = "#{::Socket.gethostname}.redmine" if host.empty?
801:       "#{hash.join('.')}@#{host}"
802:     end
803: 
804:     # Returns a Message-Id for the given object
805:     def message_id_for(object, user)
806:       token_for(object, user)
807:     end
808: 
809:     # Returns a uniq token for a given object referenced by all notifications
810:     # related to this object
811:     def references_for(object, user)
812:       token_for(object, user)
813:     end
814:   end
815: 
816:   def message_id(object)
817:     @message_id_object = object
818:   end
819: 
820:   def references(object)
821:     @references_objects ||= []
822:     @references_objects << object
823:   end
824: end

20: class Project < ApplicationRecord

36:            lambda {joins(:principal).where(:users => {:type => 'User', :status => Principal::STATUS_ACTIVE})}
37:   has_many :users, through: :members

39:   has_and_belongs_to_many :trackers, lambda {order(:position)}
40:   has_many :issues, :dependent => :destroy

172:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all projects visible by the specified user.
173:   #
174:   # Examples:
175:   #   Project.visible_condition(admin)        => "projects.status = 1"
176:   #   Project.visible_condition(normal_user)  => "((projects.status = 1) AND (projects.is_public = 1 OR projects.id IN (1,3,4)))"
177:   #   Project.visible_condition(anonymous)    => "((projects.status = 1) AND (projects.is_public = 1))"
178:   def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
179:     allowed_to_condition(user, :view_project, options)
180:   end
181: 
182:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all projects for which +user+ has the given +permission+
183:   #
184:   # Valid options:
185:   # * :skip_pre_condition => true       don't check that the module is enabled (eg. when the condition is already set elsewhere in the query)
186:   # * :project => project               limit the condition to project
187:   # * :with_subprojects => true         limit the condition to project and its subprojects
188:   # * :member => true                   limit the condition to the user projects
189:   def self.allowed_to_condition(user, permission, options={})
190:     perm = Redmine::AccessControl.permission(permission)
191:     base_statement =
192:       if perm && perm.read?
193:         "#{Project.table_name}.status IN (#{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}, #{Project::STATUS_CLOSED})"
194:       else
195:         "#{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}"
196:       end
197:     if !options[:skip_pre_condition] && perm && perm.project_module
198:       # If the permission belongs to a project module, make sure the module is enabled
199:       base_statement +=
200:         " AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 AS one FROM #{EnabledModule.table_name} em" \
201:           " WHERE em.project_id = #{Project.table_name}.id" \
202:           " AND em.name='#{perm.project_module}')"
203:     end
204:     if project = options[:project]
205:       project_statement = project.project_condition(options[:with_subprojects])
206:       base_statement = "(#{project_statement}) AND (#{base_statement})"
207:     end
208: 
209:     if user.admin?
210:       base_statement
211:     else
212:       statement_by_role = {}
213:       unless options[:member]
214:         role = user.builtin_role
215:         if role.allowed_to?(permission)
216:           s = "#{Project.table_name}.is_public = #{connection.quoted_true}"
217:           if user.id
218:             group = role.anonymous? ? Group.anonymous : Group.non_member
219:             principal_ids = [user.id, group.id].compact
220:             s =
221:               "(#{s} AND #{Project.table_name}.id NOT IN " \
222:                 "(SELECT project_id FROM #{Member.table_name} " \
223:                 "WHERE user_id IN (#{principal_ids.join(',')})))"
224:           end
225:           statement_by_role[role] = s
226:         end
227:       end
228:       user.project_ids_by_role.each do |role, project_ids|
229:         if role.allowed_to?(permission) && project_ids.any?
230:           statement_by_role[role] = "#{Project.table_name}.id IN (#{project_ids.join(',')})"
231:         end
232:       end
233:       if statement_by_role.empty?
234:         "1=0"
235:       else
236:         if block_given?
237:           statement_by_role.each do |role, statement|
238:             if s = yield(role, user)
239:               statement_by_role[role] = "(#{statement} AND (#{s}))"
240:             end
241:           end
242:         end
243:         "((#{base_statement}) AND (#{statement_by_role.values.join(' OR ')}))"
244:       end
245:     end
246:   end

248:   def override_roles(role)
249:     @override_members ||= memberships.
250:       joins(:principal).
251:       where(:users => {:type => ['GroupAnonymous', 'GroupNonMember']}).to_a
252: 
253:     group_class = role.anonymous? ? GroupAnonymous : GroupNonMember
254:     member = @override_members.detect {|m| m.principal.is_a? group_class}
255:     member ? member.roles.to_a : [role]
256:   end

269:   def activities(include_inactive=false)

271:     scope = TimeEntryActivity.where("#{t}.project_id IS NULL OR #{t}.project_id = ?", id)
272: 
273:     overridden_activity_ids = self.time_entry_activities.pluck(:parent_id).compact
274:     if overridden_activity_ids.any?
275:       scope = scope.where("#{t}.id NOT IN (?)", overridden_activity_ids)
276:     end

283:   # Creates or updates project time entry activities

286:       activities.each do |id, activity|

292:   # Will create a new Project specific Activity or update an existing one
293:   #
294:   # This will raise a ActiveRecord::Rollback if the TimeEntryActivity
295:   # does not successfully save.
296:   def update_or_create_time_entry_activity(id, activity_hash)
297:     if activity_hash.respond_to?(:has_key?) && activity_hash.has_key?('parent_id')
298:       self.create_time_entry_activity_if_needed(activity_hash)
299:     else
300:       activity = project.time_entry_activities.find_by_id(id.to_i)
301:       activity.update(activity_hash) if activity
302:     end
303:   end
304: 
305:   # Create a new TimeEntryActivity if it overrides a system TimeEntryActivity
306:   #
307:   # This will raise a ActiveRecord::Rollback if the TimeEntryActivity
308:   # does not successfully save.
309:   def create_time_entry_activity_if_needed(activity)
310:     if activity['parent_id']
311:       parent_activity = TimeEntryActivity.find(activity['parent_id'])
312:       activity['name'] = parent_activity.name
313:       activity['position'] = parent_activity.position
314:       if Enumeration.overriding_change?(activity, parent_activity)
315:         project_activity = self.time_entry_activities.create(activity)
316:         if project_activity.new_record?
317:           raise ActiveRecord::Rollback, "Overriding TimeEntryActivity was not successfully saved"
318:         else
319:           self.time_entries.
320:             where(:activity_id => parent_activity.id).
321:             update_all(:activity_id => project_activity.id)
322:         end
323:       end
324:     end
325:   end

336:   # Returns a :conditions SQL string that can be used to find the issues associated with this project.
337:   #
338:   # Examples:
339:   #   project.project_condition(true)  => "(projects.lft >= 1 AND projects.rgt <= 10)"
340:   #   project.project_condition(false) => "projects.id = 1"
341:   def project_condition(with_subprojects)
342:     if with_subprojects
343:       "(" \
344:         "#{Project.table_name}.lft >= #{lft} AND " \
345:         "#{Project.table_name}.rgt <= #{rgt}" \
346:       ")"
347:     else
348:       "#{Project.table_name}.id = #{id}"
349:     end
350:   end

357:       end
358: 
359:       project

361:       super
362:     end
363:   end

417:   # Archives the project and its descendants
418:   def archive
419:     # Check that there is no issue of a non descendant project that is assigned
420:     # to one of the project or descendant versions
421:     version_ids = self_and_descendants.joins(:versions).pluck("#{Version.table_name}.id")
422: 
423:     if version_ids.any? &&
424:       Issue.
425:         joins(:project).
426:         where("#{Project.table_name}.lft < ? OR #{Project.table_name}.rgt > ?", lft, rgt).
427:         where(:fixed_version_id => version_ids).
428:         exists?
429:       return false
430:     end
431: 
432:     Project.transaction do
433:       archive!
434:     end
435:     true
436:   end

438:   # Unarchives the project and its archived ancestors
439:   def unarchive
440:     new_status = ancestors.any?(&:closed?) ? STATUS_CLOSED : STATUS_ACTIVE
441:     self_and_ancestors.status(STATUS_ARCHIVED).update_all :status => new_status
442:     reload
443:   end
444: 
445:   def close
446:     self_and_descendants.status(STATUS_ACTIVE).update_all :status => STATUS_CLOSED
447:   end
448: 
449:   def reopen
450:     self_and_descendants.status(STATUS_CLOSED).update_all :status => STATUS_ACTIVE
451:   end

453:   # Returns an array of projects the project can be moved to
454:   # by the current user
455:   def allowed_parents(user=User.current)
456:     return @allowed_parents if @allowed_parents
457: 
458:     @allowed_parents = Project.allowed_to(user, :add_subprojects).to_a
459:     @allowed_parents = @allowed_parents - self_and_descendants
460:     if user.allowed_to?(:add_project, nil, :global => true) || (!new_record? && parent.nil?)
461:       @allowed_parents << nil
462:     end
463:     unless parent.nil? || @allowed_parents.empty? || @allowed_parents.include?(parent)
464:       @allowed_parents << parent
465:     end
466:     @allowed_parents
467:   end

504:       distinct.

529:           "#{Project.table_name}.lft >= ? AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= ?" \

534:   # Returns a scope of the Versions used by the project
535:   def shared_versions
536:     if new_record?
537:       Version.
538:         joins(:project).
539:         preload(:project).
540:         where("#{Project.table_name}.status <> ? AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing = 'system'",
541:               STATUS_ARCHIVED)
542:     else
543:       @shared_versions ||= begin
544:         r = root? ? self : root
545:         Version.
546:           joins(:project).
547:           preload(:project).
548:           where(
549:             "#{Project.table_name}.id = #{id}" \
550:             " OR (#{Project.table_name}.status <> #{Project::STATUS_ARCHIVED} AND (" \
551:             " #{Version.table_name}.sharing = 'system'" \
552:             " OR (#{Project.table_name}.lft >= #{r.lft}" \
553:             " AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= #{r.rgt}" \
554:             " AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing = 'tree')" \
555:             " OR (#{Project.table_name}.lft < #{lft}" \
556:             " AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt > #{rgt}" \
557:             " AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing IN ('hierarchy', 'descendants'))" \
558:             " OR (#{Project.table_name}.lft > #{lft}" \
559:             " AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt < #{rgt}" \
560:             " AND #{Version.table_name}.sharing = 'hierarchy')" \
561:             "))"
562:           )
563:       end
564:     end
565:   end

581:     role = self.class.default_member_role

583:     self.members << member

593:   # Deletes all project's members
594:   def delete_all_members
595:     me, mr = Member.table_name, MemberRole.table_name
596:     self.class.connection.delete(
597:       "DELETE FROM #{mr} WHERE #{mr}.member_id IN (SELECT #{me}.id FROM #{me} " \
598:         "WHERE #{me}.project_id = #{id})"
599:     )
600:     Member.where(:project_id => id).delete_all
601:   end

603:   # Return a Principal scope of users/groups issues can be assigned to
604:   def assignable_users(tracker=nil)
605:     return @assignable_users[tracker] if @assignable_users && @assignable_users[tracker]
606: 
607:     types = ['User']
608:     types << 'Group' if Setting.issue_group_assignment?
609: 
610:     scope = Principal.
611:       active.
612:       joins(:members => :roles).
613:       where(:type => types, :members => {:project_id => id}, :roles => {:assignable => true}).
614:       distinct.
615:       sorted
616: 
617:     if tracker
618:       # Rejects users that cannot the view the tracker
619:       roles =
620:         Role.where(:assignable => true).select do |role|
621:           role.permissions_tracker?(:view_issues, tracker)
622:         end
623:       scope = scope.where(:roles => {:id => roles.map(&:id)})
624:     end
625: 
626:     @assignable_users ||= {}
627:     @assignable_users[tracker] = scope
628:   end

640:   # Returns a scope of all custom fields enabled for project issues
641:   # (explicitly associated custom fields and custom fields enabled for all projects)
642:   def all_issue_custom_fields
643:     if new_record?
644:       @all_issue_custom_fields ||= IssueCustomField.
645:         sorted.
646:         where("is_for_all = ? OR id IN (?)", true, issue_custom_field_ids)
647:     else
648:       @all_issue_custom_fields ||= IssueCustomField.
649:         sorted.
650:         where("is_for_all = ? OR id IN (SELECT DISTINCT cfp.custom_field_id" +
651:           " FROM #{table_name_prefix}custom_fields_projects#{table_name_suffix} cfp" +
652:           " WHERE cfp.project_id = ?)", true, id)
653:     end
654:   end

656:   # Returns a scope of all custom fields enabled for issues of the project
657:   # and its subprojects
658:   def rolled_up_custom_fields
659:     if leaf?
660:       all_issue_custom_fields
661:     else
662:       @rolled_up_custom_fields ||= IssueCustomField.
663:         sorted.
664:         where("is_for_all = ? OR EXISTS (SELECT 1" +
665:           " FROM #{table_name_prefix}custom_fields_projects#{table_name_suffix} cfp" +
666:           " JOIN #{Project.table_name} p ON p.id = cfp.project_id" +
667:           " WHERE cfp.custom_field_id = #{CustomField.table_name}.id" +
668:           " AND p.lft >= ? AND p.rgt <= ?)", true, lft, rgt)
669:     end
670:   end

731:   # Returns the percent completed for this project, based on the
732:   # progress on it's versions.
733:   def completed_percent(options={:include_subprojects => false})
734:     if options.delete(:include_subprojects)
735:       total = self_and_descendants.sum(&:completed_percent)
736: 
737:       total / self_and_descendants.count
738:     else
739:       if versions.count > 0
740:         total = versions.sum(&:completed_percent)
741: 
742:         total / versions.count
743:       else
744:         100
745:       end
746:     end
747:   end

749:   # Return true if this project allows to do the specified action.
750:   # action can be:
751:   # * a parameter-like Hash (eg. :controller => 'projects', :action => 'edit')
752:   # * a permission Symbol (eg. :edit_project)
753:   def allows_to?(action)
754:     if archived?
755:       # No action allowed on archived projects
756:       return false
757:     end
758:     unless active? || Redmine::AccessControl.read_action?(action)
759:       # No write action allowed on closed projects
760:       return false
761:     end
762: 
763:     # No action allowed on disabled modules
764:     if action.is_a? Hash
765:       allowed_actions.include? "#{action[:controller]}/#{action[:action]}"
766:     else
767:       allowed_permissions.include? action
768:     end
769:   end

791:   def enabled_module_names=(module_names)
792:     if module_names && module_names.is_a?(Array)
793:       module_names = module_names.collect(&:to_s).reject(&:blank?)
794:       self.enabled_modules =
795:         module_names.collect do |name|
796:           enabled_modules.detect {|mod| mod.name == name} ||
797:             EnabledModule.new(:name => name)
798:         end
799:     else
800:       enabled_modules.clear

809:   # Enable a specific module
810:   #
811:   # Examples:
812:   #   project.enable_module!(:issue_tracking)
813:   #   project.enable_module!("issue_tracking")
814:   def enable_module!(name)
815:     enabled_modules << EnabledModule.new(:name => name.to_s) unless module_enabled?(name)
816:   end
817: 
818:   # Disable a module if it exists
819:   #
820:   # Examples:
821:   #   project.disable_module!(:issue_tracking)
822:   #   project.disable_module!("issue_tracking")
823:   #   project.disable_module!(project.enabled_modules.first)
824:   def disable_module!(target)
825:     target = enabled_modules.detect{|mod| target.to_s == mod.name} unless enabled_modules.include?(target)
826:     (target.presence&.destroy)
827:   end

879:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
880:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
881:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
882:     end
883:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
884: 
885:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
886: 
887:     @unallowed_parent_id = nil
888:     if new_record? || attrs.key?('parent_id')
889:       parent_id_param = attrs['parent_id'].to_s
890:       if new_record? || parent_id_param != parent_id.to_s
891:         p = parent_id_param.present? ? Project.find_by_id(parent_id_param) : nil
892:         unless allowed_parents(user).include?(p)
893:           attrs.delete('parent_id')
894:           @unallowed_parent_id = true
895:         end
896:       end
897:     end
898: 
899:     # Reject custom fields values not visible by the user
900:     if attrs['custom_field_values'].present?
901:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
902:       attrs['custom_field_values'].reject! {|k, v| !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(k.to_s)}
903:     end
904: 
905:     # Reject custom fields not visible by the user
906:     if attrs['custom_fields'].present?
907:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
908:       attrs['custom_fields'].reject! {|c| !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c['id'].to_s)}
909:     end
910: 
911:     super
912:   end

920:   # Copies and saves the Project instance based on the +project+.
921:   # Duplicates the source project's:
922:   # * Wiki
923:   # * Versions
924:   # * Categories
925:   # * Issues
926:   # * Members
927:   # * Queries
928:   #
929:   # Accepts an +options+ argument to specify what to copy
930:   #
931:   # Examples:
932:   #   project.copy(1)                                    # => copies everything
933:   #   project.copy(1, :only => 'members')                # => copies members only
934:   #   project.copy(1, :only => ['members', 'versions'])  # => copies members and versions
935:   def copy(project, options={})
936:     project = Project.find(project) unless project.is_a?(Project)
937: 
938:     to_be_copied = %w(members wiki versions issue_categories issues queries boards documents)
939:     to_be_copied = to_be_copied & Array.wrap(options[:only]) unless options[:only].nil?
940: 
941:     Project.transaction do
942:       if save
943:         reload
944: 
945:         self.attachments = project.attachments.map do |attachment|
946:           attachment.copy(:container => self)
947:         end
948: 
949:         to_be_copied.each do |name|
950:           send :"copy_#{name}", project
951:         end
952:         Redmine::Hook.call_hook(:model_project_copy_before_save,
953:                                 :source_project => project,
954:                                 :destination_project => self)
955:         save
956:       else
957:         false
958:       end
959:     end
960:   end
961: 
962:   # Returns a new unsaved Project instance with attributes copied from +project+
963:   def self.copy_from(project)
964:     project = Project.find(project) unless project.is_a?(Project)
965:     # clear unique attributes
966:     attributes =
967:       project.attributes.dup.except('id', 'name', 'identifier',
968:                                     'status', 'parent_id', 'lft', 'rgt')
969:     copy = Project.new(attributes)
970:     copy.enabled_module_names = project.enabled_module_names
971:     copy.trackers = project.trackers
972:     copy.custom_values = project.custom_values.collect {|v| v.clone}
973:     copy.issue_custom_fields = project.issue_custom_fields
974:     copy
975:   end

977:   # Yields the given block for each project with its level in the tree
978:   def self.project_tree(projects, options={}, &block)
979:     ancestors = []
980:     if options[:init_level] && projects.first
981:       ancestors = projects.first.ancestors.to_a
982:     end
983:     projects.sort_by(&:lft).each do |project|
984:       while ancestors.any? &&
985:              !project.is_descendant_of?(ancestors.last)
986:         ancestors.pop
987:       end
988:       yield project, ancestors.size
989:       ancestors << project
990:     end
991:   end

996:   # to edit this field).

1032:   def update_inherited_members
1033:     if parent
1034:       if inherit_members? && !inherit_members_before_last_save
1035:         remove_inherited_member_roles
1036:         add_inherited_member_roles
1037:       elsif !inherit_members? && inherit_members_before_last_save
1038:         remove_inherited_member_roles
1039:       end
1040:     end
1041:   end
1042: 
1043:   def remove_inherited_member_roles
1044:     member_roles = MemberRole.where(:member_id => membership_ids).to_a
1045:     member_role_ids = member_roles.map(&:id)
1046:     member_roles.each do |member_role|
1047:       if member_role.inherited_from && !member_role_ids.include?(member_role.inherited_from)
1048:         member_role.destroy
1049:       end
1050:     end
1051:   end
1052: 
1053:   def add_inherited_member_roles
1054:     if inherit_members? && parent
1055:       parent.memberships.each do |parent_member|
1056:         member = Member.find_or_initialize_by(:project_id => self.id, :user_id => parent_member.user_id)
1057:         parent_member.member_roles.each do |parent_member_role|
1058:           member.member_roles <<
1059:             MemberRole.new(:role => parent_member_role.role,
1060:                            :inherited_from => parent_member_role.id)
1061:         end
1062:         member.save!
1063:       end
1064:       memberships.reset
1065:     end
1066:   end

1082:   # Copies wiki from +project+
1083:   def copy_wiki(project)
1084:     # Check that the source project has a wiki first
1085:     unless project.wiki.nil?
1086:       wiki = self.wiki || Wiki.new
1087:       wiki.attributes = project.wiki.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id")
1088:       wiki_pages_map = {}
1089:       project.wiki.pages.each do |page|
1090:         # Skip pages without content
1091:         next if page.content.nil?
1092: 
1093:         new_wiki_content =
1094:           WikiContent.new(page.content.attributes.dup.except("id", "page_id", "updated_on"))
1095:         new_wiki_page =
1096:           WikiPage.new(page.attributes.dup.except("id", "wiki_id", "created_on", "parent_id"))
1097:         new_wiki_page.content = new_wiki_content
1098:         wiki.pages << new_wiki_page
1099:         new_wiki_page.attachments =
1100:           page.attachments.map{|attachement| attachement.copy(:container => new_wiki_page)}
1101:         wiki_pages_map[page.id] = new_wiki_page
1102:       end
1103: 
1104:       self.wiki = wiki
1105:       wiki.save
1106:       # Reproduce page hierarchy
1107:       project.wiki.pages.each do |page|
1108:         if page.parent_id && wiki_pages_map[page.id]
1109:           wiki_pages_map[page.id].parent = wiki_pages_map[page.parent_id]
1110:           wiki_pages_map[page.id].save
1111:         end
1112:       end
1113:     end
1114:   end
1115: 
1116:   # Copies versions from +project+
1117:   def copy_versions(project)
1118:     project.versions.each do |version|
1119:       new_version = Version.new
1120:       new_version.attributes =
1121:         version.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id", "created_on", "updated_on")
1122: 
1123:       new_version.attachments = version.attachments.map do |attachment|
1124:         attachment.copy(:container => new_version)
1125:       end
1126: 
1127:       self.versions << new_version
1128:     end
1129:   end

1131:   # Copies issue categories from +project+
1132:   def copy_issue_categories(project)
1133:     project.issue_categories.each do |issue_category|
1134:       new_issue_category = IssueCategory.new
1135:       new_issue_category.attributes = issue_category.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id")
1136:       self.issue_categories << new_issue_category
1137:     end
1138:   end

1140:   # Copies issues from +project+
1141:   def copy_issues(project)
1142:     # Stores the source issue id as a key and the copied issues as the
1143:     # value.  Used to map the two together for issue relations.
1144:     issues_map = {}
1145: 
1146:     # Store status and reopen locked/closed versions
1147:     version_statuses = versions.reject(&:open?).map {|version| [version, version.status]}
1148:     version_statuses.each do |version, _| # rubocop:disable Style/HashEachMethods
1149:       version.update_attribute :status, 'open'
1150:     end
1151: 
1152:     # Get issues sorted by root_id, lft so that parent issues
1153:     # get copied before their children
1154:     project.issues.reorder('root_id, lft').each do |issue|
1155:       new_issue = Issue.new
1156:       new_issue.copy_from(issue, :subtasks => false, :link => false, :keep_status => true)
1157:       new_issue.project = self
1158:       # Changing project resets the custom field values
1159:       # TODO: handle this in Issue#project=
1160:       new_issue.custom_field_values = issue.custom_field_values.to_h do |v|
1161:         [v.custom_field_id, v.value]
1162:       end
1163:       # Reassign fixed_versions by name, since names are unique per project
1164:       if issue.fixed_version && issue.fixed_version.project == project
1165:         new_issue.fixed_version = self.versions.detect {|v| v.name == issue.fixed_version.name}
1166:       end
1167:       # Reassign version custom field values
1168:       new_issue.custom_field_values.each do |custom_value|
1169:         if custom_value.custom_field.field_format == 'version' && custom_value.value.present?
1170:           versions = Version.where(:id => custom_value.value).to_a
1171:           new_value = versions.map do |version|
1172:             if version.project == project
1173:               self.versions.detect {|v| v.name == version.name}.try(:id)
1174:             else
1175:               version.id
1176:             end
1177:           end
1178:           new_value.compact!
1179:           new_value = new_value.first unless custom_value.custom_field.multiple?
1180:           custom_value.value = new_value
1181:         end
1182:       end
1183:       # Reassign the category by name, since names are unique per project
1184:       if issue.category
1185:         new_issue.category = self.issue_categories.detect {|c| c.name == issue.category.name}
1186:       end
1187:       # Parent issue
1188:       if issue.parent_id
1189:         if copied_parent = issues_map[issue.parent_id]
1190:           new_issue.parent_issue_id = copied_parent.id
1191:         end
1192:       end
1193: 
1194:       self.issues << new_issue
1195:       if new_issue.new_record?
1196:         if logger && logger.info?
1197:           logger.info(
1198:             "Project#copy_issues: issue ##{issue.id} could not be copied: " \
1199:               "#{new_issue.errors.full_messages}"
1200:           )
1201:         end
1202:       else
1203:         issues_map[issue.id] = new_issue unless new_issue.new_record?
1204:       end
1205:     end
1206: 
1207:     # Restore locked/closed version statuses
1208:     version_statuses.each do |version, status|
1209:       version.update_attribute :status, status
1210:     end
1211: 
1212:     # Relations after in case issues related each other
1213:     project.issues.each do |issue|
1214:       new_issue = issues_map[issue.id]
1215:       unless new_issue
1216:         # Issue was not copied
1217:         next
1218:       end
1219: 
1220:       # Relations
1221:       issue.relations_from.each do |source_relation|
1222:         new_issue_relation = IssueRelation.new
1223:         new_issue_relation.attributes =
1224:           source_relation.attributes.dup.except("id", "issue_from_id", "issue_to_id")
1225:         new_issue_relation.issue_to = issues_map[source_relation.issue_to_id]
1226:         if new_issue_relation.issue_to.nil? && Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
1227:           new_issue_relation.issue_to = source_relation.issue_to
1228:         end
1229:         new_issue.relations_from << new_issue_relation
1230:       end
1231: 
1232:       issue.relations_to.each do |source_relation|
1233:         new_issue_relation = IssueRelation.new
1234:         new_issue_relation.attributes =
1235:           source_relation.attributes.dup.except("id", "issue_from_id", "issue_to_id")
1236:         new_issue_relation.issue_from = issues_map[source_relation.issue_from_id]
1237:         if new_issue_relation.issue_from.nil? && Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
1238:           new_issue_relation.issue_from = source_relation.issue_from
1239:         end
1240:         new_issue.relations_to << new_issue_relation
1241:       end
1242:     end
1243:   end
1244: 
1245:   # Copies members from +project+
1246:   def copy_members(project)
1247:     # Copy users first, then groups to handle members with inherited and given roles
1248:     user_memberships, group_memberships =
1249:       project.memberships.partition {|m| m.principal.is_a?(User)}
1250:     members_to_copy = user_memberships + group_memberships
1251: 
1252:     members_to_copy.each do |member|
1253:       new_member = Member.new
1254:       new_member.attributes = member.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id", "created_on")
1255:       # only copy non inherited roles
1256:       # inherited roles will be added when copying the group membership
1257:       role_ids = member.member_roles.reject(&:inherited?).collect(&:role_id)
1258:       next if role_ids.empty?
1259: 
1260:       new_member.role_ids = role_ids
1261:       new_member.project = self
1262:       self.members << new_member
1263:     end
1264:   end
1265: 
1266:   # Copies queries from +project+
1267:   def copy_queries(project)
1268:     project.queries.each do |query|
1269:       new_query = query.class.new
1270:       new_query.attributes =
1271:         query.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id", "sort_criteria",
1272:                                     "user_id", "type")
1273:       new_query.sort_criteria = query.sort_criteria if query.sort_criteria
1274:       new_query.project = self
1275:       new_query.user_id = query.user_id
1276:       new_query.role_ids = query.role_ids if query.visibility == ::Query::VISIBILITY_ROLES
1277:       self.queries << new_query
1278:       if query == project.default_issue_query
1279:         self.default_issue_query = new_query
1280:       end
1281:     end
1282:   end
1283: 
1284:   # Copies boards from +project+
1285:   def copy_boards(project)
1286:     project.boards.each do |board|
1287:       new_board = Board.new
1288:       new_board.attributes =
1289:         board.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id", "topics_count",
1290:                                     "messages_count", "last_message_id")
1291:       new_board.project = self
1292:       self.boards << new_board
1293:     end
1294:   end
1295: 
1296:   # Copies documents from +project+
1297:   def copy_documents(project)
1298:     project.documents.each do |document|
1299:       new_document = Document.new
1300:       new_document.attributes = document.attributes.dup.except("id", "project_id")
1301:       new_document.project = self
1302: 
1303:       new_document.attachments = document.attachments.map do |attachement|
1304:         attachement.copy(:container => new_document)
1305:       end
1306: 
1307:       self.documents << new_document
1308:     end
1309:   end
1310: 
1311:   def allowed_permissions
1312:     @allowed_permissions ||= begin
1313:       module_names =
1314:         if enabled_modules.loaded?
1315:           enabled_modules.map(&:name)
1316:         else
1317:           enabled_modules.pluck(:name)
1318:         end
1319:       Redmine::AccessControl.modules_permissions(module_names).collect {|p| p.name}
1320:     end
1321:   end
1322: 
1323:   def allowed_actions
1324:     @actions_allowed ||= allowed_permissions.inject([]) do |actions, permission|
1325:       actions += Redmine::AccessControl.allowed_actions(permission)
1326:     end.flatten
1327:   end
1328: 
1329:   # Archives subprojects recursively
1330:   def archive!
1331:     children.each do |subproject|
1332:       subproject.send :archive!
1333:     end
1334:     update_attribute :status, STATUS_ARCHIVED
1335:   end
1336: 
1337:   def fetch_last_activity_date
1338:     latest_activities = Redmine::Activity::Fetcher.new(User.current, :project => self).events(nil, nil, :last_by_project => true)
1339:     latest_activities.empty? ? nil : latest_activities.to_h[self.id]
1340:   end
1341: end

109:   def link_to_issue(issue, options={})
110:     title = nil
111:     subject = nil
112:     text = options[:tracker] == false ? "##{issue.id}" : "#{issue.tracker} ##{issue.id}"
113:     if options[:subject] == false

122:     s = link_to(text, issue_url(issue, :only_path => only_path),
123:                 :class => issue.css_classes, :title => title)
124:     s << h(": #{subject}") if subject

20: class SearchController < ApplicationController
21:   before_action :find_optional_project_by_id, :authorize_global
22:   accept_api_auth :index
23: 
24:   def index
25:     @question = params[:q]&.strip || ""
26:     @all_words = params[:all_words] ? params[:all_words].present? : true
27:     @titles_only = params[:titles_only] ? params[:titles_only].present? : false
28:     @search_attachments = params[:attachments].presence || '0'
29:     @open_issues = params[:open_issues] ? params[:open_issues].present? : false
30: 
31:     case params[:format]
32:     when 'xml', 'json'
33:       @offset, @limit = api_offset_and_limit
34:     else
35:       @offset = nil
36:       @limit = Setting.search_results_per_page.to_i
37:       @limit = 10 if @limit == 0
38:     end
39: 
40:     # quick jump to an issue
41:     if !api_request? && (m = @question.match(/^#?(\d+)$/)) && (issue = Issue.visible.find_by_id(m[1].to_i))
42:       redirect_to issue_path(issue)
43:       return
44:     end
45: 
46:     projects_to_search =
47:       case params[:scope]
48:       when 'all'
49:         nil
50:       when 'my_projects'
51:         User.current.projects
52:       when 'bookmarks'
53:         Project.where(id: User.current.bookmarked_project_ids)
54:       when 'subprojects'
55:         @project ? (@project.self_and_descendants.to_a) : nil
56:       else
57:         @project
58:       end
59: 
60:     @object_types = Redmine::Search.available_search_types.dup
61:     if projects_to_search.is_a? Project
62:       # don't search projects
63:       @object_types.delete('projects')
64:       # only show what the user is allowed to view
65:       @object_types = @object_types.select {|o| User.current.allowed_to?(:"view_#{o}", projects_to_search)}
66:     end
67: 
68:     @scope = @object_types.select {|t| params[t].present?}
69:     @scope = @object_types if @scope.empty?
70: 
71:     fetcher = Redmine::Search::Fetcher.new(
72:       @question, User.current, @scope, projects_to_search,
73:       :all_words => @all_words, :titles_only => @titles_only, :attachments => @search_attachments, :open_issues => @open_issues,
74:       :cache => params[:page].present?, :params => params.to_unsafe_hash
75:     )
76: 
77:     if fetcher.tokens.present?
78:       @result_count = fetcher.result_count
79:       @result_count_by_type = fetcher.result_count_by_type
80:       @tokens = fetcher.tokens
81: 
82:       @result_pages = Paginator.new @result_count, @limit, params['page']
83:       @offset ||= @result_pages.offset
84:       @results = fetcher.results(@offset, @result_pages.per_page)
85:     else
86:       @question = ""
87:     end
88:     respond_to do |format|
89:       format.html {render :layout => false if request.xhr?}
90:       format.api do
91:         @results ||= []
92:         render :layout => false
93:       end
94:     end
95:   end
96: end

20: module GanttHelper
21:   def gantt_zoom_link(gantt, in_or_out)
22:     case in_or_out
23:     when :in
24:       if gantt.zoom < 4
25:         link_to(
26:           sprite_icon('zoom-in', l(:text_zoom_in)),
27:           {:params => request.query_parameters.merge(gantt.params.merge(:zoom => (gantt.zoom + 1)))},
28:           :class => 'icon icon-zoom-in')
29:       else
30:         content_tag(:span, sprite_icon('zoom-in', l(:text_zoom_in)), :class => 'icon icon-zoom-in').html_safe
31:       end
32: 
33:     when :out
34:       if gantt.zoom > 1
35:         link_to(
36:           sprite_icon('zoom-out', l(:text_zoom_out)),
37:           {:params => request.query_parameters.merge(gantt.params.merge(:zoom => (gantt.zoom - 1)))},
38:           :class => 'icon icon-zoom-out')
39:       else
40:         content_tag(:span, sprite_icon('zoom-out', l(:text_zoom_out)), :class => 'icon icon-zoom-out').html_safe
41:       end
42:     end
43:   end
44: 
45:   def gantt_chart_tag(query, &)
46:     data_attributes = {
47:       controller: 'gantt--chart',
48:       # Events emitted by child controllers the chart listens to.
49:       # - `gantt--options` toggles checkboxes under Options.
50:       # - `gantt--subjects` reports tree expand/collapse.
51:       # - Window resize triggers a redraw of progress lines and relations.
52:       action: %w(
53:         gantt--options:toggle-display@document->gantt--chart#handleOptionsDisplay
54:         gantt--options:toggle-relations@document->gantt--chart#handleOptionsRelations
55:         gantt--options:toggle-progress@document->gantt--chart#handleOptionsProgress
56:         gantt--subjects:toggle-tree->gantt--chart#handleSubjectTreeChanged
57:         resize@window->gantt--chart#handleWindowResize
58:       ).join(' '),
59:       'gantt--chart-issue-relation-types-value': Redmine::Helpers::Gantt::DRAW_TYPES.to_json,
60:       'gantt--chart-show-selected-columns-value': query.draw_selected_columns ? 'true' : 'false',
61:       'gantt--chart-show-relations-value': query.draw_relations ? 'true' : 'false',
62:       'gantt--chart-show-progress-value': query.draw_progress_line ? 'true' : 'false'
63:     }
64: 
65:     tag.table(class: 'gantt-table', data: data_attributes, &)
66:   end
67: 
68:   def gantt_column_tag(column_name, min_width: nil, **options, &)
69:     options[:data] = {
70:       controller: 'gantt--column',
71:       action: 'resize@window->gantt--column#handleWindowResize',
72:       'gantt--column-min-width-value': min_width,
73:       'gantt--column-column-value': column_name
74:     }
75:     options[:class] = ["gantt_#{column_name}_column", options[:class]]
76: 
77:     tag.td(**options, &)
78:   end
79: 
80:   def gantt_subjects_tag(&)
81:     data_attributes = {
82:       controller: 'gantt--subjects',
83:       action: 'gantt--column:resize-column-subjects@document->gantt--subjects#handleResizeColumn'
84:     }
85:     tag.div(class: "gantt_subjects", data: data_attributes, &)
86:   end

29:   # Returns the action links for an issue journal
30:   def render_journal_actions(issue, journal, options={})
31:     links = []
32:     dropbown_links = []
33:     indice = journal.indice || @journal.issue.visible_journals_with_index.find{|j| j.id == @journal.id}.indice
34: 
35:     dropbown_links << copy_object_url_link(issue_url(issue, anchor: "note-#{indice}", only_path: false))

37:       dropbown_links << link_to(sprite_icon('download', l(:label_download_all_attachments)),
38:                                 container_attachments_download_path(journal),
39:                                 :title => l(:label_download_all_attachments),
40:                                 :class => 'icon icon-download'
41:                                )
42:     end
43: 
44:     links << reaction_button(journal)
45: 
46:     if journal.notes.present?
47:       if options[:reply_links]
48:         url = quoted_issue_path(issue, :journal_id => journal, :journal_indice => indice)
49:         links << quote_reply_button(url: url, icon_only: true)
50:       end
51:       if journal.editable_by?(User.current)
52:         links << link_to(sprite_icon('edit', l(:button_edit)),
53:                          edit_journal_path(journal),

58:                         )
59:         dropbown_links << link_to(sprite_icon('del', l(:button_delete)),

62:                                   :method => 'put',

64:                                   :class => 'icon icon-del'
65:                                  )
66:       end
67:     end
68:     safe_join(links, ' ') + actions_dropdown {safe_join(dropbown_links, ' ')}

20: module FixedIssuesExtension
21:   # Returns the total estimated time for this version
22:   # (sum of leaves estimated_hours)
23:   def estimated_hours
24:     @estimated_hours ||= sum(:estimated_hours).to_f
25:   end
26: 
27:   # Returns the total estimated remaining time for this version
28:   # (sum of leaves remaining_estimated_hours)
29:   def estimated_remaining_hours
30:     @estimated_remaining_hours ||= sum(IssueQuery::ESTIMATED_REMAINING_HOURS_SQL).to_f
31:   end
32: 
33:   # Returns the total amount of open issues for this version.
34:   def open_count
35:     load_counts
36:     @open_count
37:   end
38: 
39:   # Returns the total amount of closed issues for this version.
40:   def closed_count
41:     load_counts
42:     @closed_count
43:   end
44: 
45:   # Returns the completion percentage of this version based on the amount of open/closed issues
46:   # and the time spent on the open issues.
47:   def completed_percent
48:     return 0 if open_count + closed_count == 0
49:     return 100 if open_count == 0
50: 
51:     issues_progress(false) + issues_progress(true)
52:   end
53: 
54:   # Returns the percentage of issues that have been marked as 'closed'.
55:   def closed_percent
56:     return 0 if open_count + closed_count == 0
57:     return 100 if open_count == 0
58: 
59:     issues_progress(false)
60:   end
61: 
62:   private
63: 
64:   def load_counts
65:     unless @open_count
66:       @open_count = 0
67:       @closed_count = 0
68:       self.group(:status).count.each do |status, count|
69:         if status.is_closed?
70:           @closed_count += count
71:         else
72:           @open_count += count
73:         end
74:       end
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   # Returns the average estimated time of assigned issues
79:   # or 1 if no issue has an estimated time
80:   # Used to weight unestimated issues in progress calculation
81:   def estimated_average
82:     if @estimated_average.nil?
83:       issues_with_total_estimated_hours = select {|c| c.total_estimated_hours.to_f > 0.0}
84:       if issues_with_total_estimated_hours.any?
85:         average = issues_with_total_estimated_hours.sum(&:total_estimated_hours).to_f / issues_with_total_estimated_hours.count
86:       else
87:         average = 1.0
88:       end
89:       @estimated_average = average
90:     end
91:     @estimated_average
92:   end
93: 
94:   # Returns the total progress of open or closed issues.  The returned percentage takes into account
95:   # the amount of estimated time set for this version.
96:   #
97:   # Examples:
98:   # issues_progress(true)   => returns the progress percentage for open issues.
99:   # issues_progress(false)  => returns the progress percentage for closed issues.
100:   def issues_progress(open)
101:     @issues_progress ||= {}
102:     @issues_progress[open] ||= begin
103:       progress = 0
104:       issues_count = open_count + closed_count
105:       if issues_count > 0
106:         done = self.open(open).sum do |c|
107:           estimated = c.total_estimated_hours.to_f
108:           estimated = estimated_average unless estimated > 0.0
109:           ratio = open ? (c.done_ratio || 0) : 100
110:           estimated * ratio
111:         end
112:         progress = done / (estimated_average * issues_count)
113:       end
114:       progress
115:     end
116:   end
117: end
118: 
119: class Version < ApplicationRecord
120:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
121: 
122:   after_update :update_issues_from_sharing_change
123:   before_destroy :nullify_projects_default_version
124:   after_save :update_default_project_version
125: 
126:   belongs_to :project
127:   has_many :fixed_issues, :class_name => 'Issue', :foreign_key => 'fixed_version_id', :dependent => :nullify, :extend => FixedIssuesExtension
128: 
129:   acts_as_customizable
130:   acts_as_attachable :view_permission => :view_files,
131:                      :edit_permission => :manage_files,
132:                      :delete_permission => :manage_files
133:   acts_as_webhookable
134: 
135:   VERSION_STATUSES = %w(open locked closed)
136:   VERSION_SHARINGS = %w(none descendants hierarchy tree system)
137: 
138:   validates_presence_of :name
139:   validates_uniqueness_of :name, :scope => [:project_id], :case_sensitive => true
140:   validates_length_of :name, :maximum => 60
141:   validates_length_of :description, :wiki_page_title, :maximum => 255
142:   validates :effective_date, :date => true
143:   validates_inclusion_of :status, :in => VERSION_STATUSES
144:   validates_inclusion_of :sharing, :in => VERSION_SHARINGS
145: 
146:   scope :named, lambda {|arg| where("LOWER(#{table_name}.name) = LOWER(?)", arg.to_s.strip)}
147:   scope :like, (lambda do |arg|
148:     if arg.present?
149:       pattern = "%#{sanitize_sql_like arg.to_s.strip}%"
150:       where([Redmine::Database.like("#{Version.table_name}.name", '?'), pattern])
151:     end
152:   end)
153:   scope :open, lambda {where(:status => 'open')}
154:   scope :status, (lambda do |status|
155:     if status.present?
156:       where(:status => status.to_s)
157:     end
158:   end)
159:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
160:     joins(:project).
161:     where(Project.allowed_to_condition(args.first || User.current, :view_issues))
162:   end)
163: 
164:   safe_attributes 'name',
165:                   'description',
166:                   'effective_date',
167:                   'due_date',
168:                   'wiki_page_title',
169:                   'status',
170:                   'sharing',
171:                   'default_project_version',
172:                   'custom_field_values',
173:                   'custom_fields'
174: 
175:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
176:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
177:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
178:     end
179:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
180: 
181:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
182:     # Reject custom fields values not visible by the user
183:     if attrs['custom_field_values'].present?
184:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
185:       attrs['custom_field_values'].reject! {|k, v| !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(k.to_s)}
186:     end
187: 
188:     # Reject custom fields not visible by the user
189:     if attrs['custom_fields'].present?
190:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
191:       attrs['custom_fields'].reject! {|c| !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c['id'].to_s)}
192:     end
193: 
194:     super
195:   end
196: 
197:   # Returns true if +user+ or current user is allowed to view the version
198:   def visible?(user=User.current)
199:     user.allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project)
200:   end
201: 
202:   # Returns the custom_field_values that can be edited by the given user
203:   def editable_custom_field_values(user=nil)
204:     visible_custom_field_values(user)
205:   end
206: 
207:   def visible_custom_field_values(user = nil)
208:     user ||= User.current
209:     custom_field_values.select do |value|
210:       value.custom_field.visible_by?(project, user)
211:     end
212:   end
213: 
214:   # Version files have same visibility as project files
215:   def attachments_visible?(*)
216:     project.present? && project.attachments_visible?(*)
217:   end
218: 
219:   def attachments_deletable?(usr=User.current)
220:     project.present? && project.attachments_deletable?(usr)
221:   end
222: 
223:   alias :base_reload :reload
224:   def reload(*)
225:     @default_project_version = nil
226:     @visible_fixed_issues = nil
227:     base_reload(*)
228:   end
229: 
230:   def start_date
231:     @start_date ||= fixed_issues.minimum('start_date')
232:   end
233: 
234:   def due_date
235:     effective_date
236:   end
237: 
238:   def due_date=(arg)
239:     self.effective_date=(arg)
240:   end
241: 
242:   # Returns the total estimated time for this version
243:   # (sum of leaves estimated_hours)
244:   def estimated_hours
245:     fixed_issues.estimated_hours
246:   end
247: 
248:   # Returns the total estimated remaining time for this version
249:   # (sum of leaves estimated_remaining_hours)
250:   def estimated_remaining_hours
251:     @estimated_remaining_hours ||= fixed_issues.estimated_remaining_hours
252:   end
253: 
254:   # Returns the total reported time for this version
255:   def spent_hours
256:     @spent_hours ||= TimeEntry.joins(:issue).where("#{Issue.table_name}.fixed_version_id = ?", id).sum(:hours).to_f
257:   end
258: 
259:   def closed?
260:     status == 'closed'
261:   end
262: 
263:   def open?
264:     status == 'open'
265:   end
266: 
267:   # Returns true if the version is completed: closed or due date reached and no open issues
268:   def completed?
269:     closed? || (effective_date && (effective_date < User.current.today) && (open_issues_count == 0))
270:   end
271: 
272:   def behind_schedule?
273:     # Blank due date, no issues, or 100% completed, so it's not late
274:     return false if due_date.nil? || start_date.nil? || completed_percent == 100
275: 
276:     done_date = start_date + ((due_date - start_date + 1) * completed_percent / 100).floor
277:     done_date <= User.current.today
278:   end
279: 
280:   # Returns the completion percentage of this version based on the amount of open/closed issues
281:   # and the time spent on the open issues.
282:   def completed_percent
283:     fixed_issues.completed_percent
284:   end
285: 
286:   # Returns the percentage of issues that have been marked as 'closed'.
287:   def closed_percent
288:     fixed_issues.closed_percent
289:   end
290: 
291:   # Returns true if the version is overdue: due date reached and some open issues
292:   def overdue?
293:     effective_date && (effective_date < User.current.today) && (open_issues_count > 0)
294:   end
295: 
296:   # Returns assigned issues count
297:   def issues_count
298:     fixed_issues.count
299:   end

20: module Redmine
21:   # Exception raised when a plugin cannot be found given its id.
22:   class PluginNotFound < StandardError; end
23: 
24:   # Exception raised when a plugin requirement is not met.
25:   class PluginRequirementError < StandardError; end
26: 
27:   # Base class for Redmine plugins.
28:   # Plugins are registered using the <tt>register</tt> class method that acts as the public constructor.
29:   #
30:   #   Redmine::Plugin.register :example do
31:   #     name 'Example plugin'
32:   #     author 'John Smith'
33:   #     description 'This is an example plugin for Redmine'
34:   #     version '0.0.1'
35:   #     settings :default => {'foo'=>'bar'}, :partial => 'settings/settings'
36:   #   end
37:   #
38:   # === Plugin attributes
39:   #
40:   # +settings+ is an optional attribute that let the plugin be configurable.
41:   # It must be a hash with the following keys:
42:   # * <tt>:default</tt>: default value for the plugin settings
43:   # * <tt>:partial</tt>: path of the configuration partial view, relative to the plugin <tt>app/views</tt> directory
44:   # Example:
45:   #   settings :default => {'foo'=>'bar'}, :partial => 'settings/settings'
46:   # In this example, the settings partial will be found here in the plugin directory: <tt>app/views/settings/_settings.rhtml</tt>.
47:   #
48:   # When rendered, the plugin settings value is available as the local variable +settings+
49:   #
50:   # See: http://www.redmine.org/projects/redmine/wiki/Plugin_Tutorial
51:   class Plugin
52:     # Absolute path to the directory where plugins are located
53:     cattr_accessor :directory
54:     self.directory = PluginLoader.directory
55: 
56:     # Absolute path to the public directory where plugins assets are copied
57:     cattr_accessor :public_directory
58:     self.public_directory = PluginLoader.public_directory
59: 
60:     @registered_plugins = {}
61:     @used_partials = {}
62: 
63:     attr_accessor :path
64: 
65:     class << self
66:       attr_reader :registered_plugins
67:       private :new
68: 
69:       def def_field(*names)
70:         class_eval do
71:           names.each do |name|
72:             define_method(name) do |*args|
73:               args.empty? ? instance_variable_get(:"@#{name}") : instance_variable_set(:"@#{name}", *args)
74:             end
75:           end
76:         end
77:       end
78:     end
79:     def_field :name, :description, :url, :author, :author_url, :version, :settings, :directory
80:     attr_reader :id
81: 
82:     # Plugin constructor: instanciates a new Redmine::Plugin with given +id+
83:     # and make it evaluate the given +block+
84:     #
85:     # Example
86:     #   Redmine::Plugin.register :example do
87:     #     name 'Example plugin'
88:     #     author 'John Smith'
89:     #     description 'This is an example plugin for Redmine'
90:     #     version '0.0.1'
91:     #     requires_redmine version_or_higher: '3.0.0'
92:     #   end
93:     def self.register(id, &)
94:       p = new(id)
95:       p.instance_eval(&)
96: 
97:       # Set a default name if it was not provided during registration
98:       p.name(id.to_s.humanize) if p.name.nil?
99:       # Set a default directory if it was not provided during registration
100:       p.directory(File.join(self.directory, id.to_s)) if p.directory.nil?

20: module Redmine
21:   module Hook
22:     @@listener_classes = []
23:     @@listeners = nil
24:     @@hook_listeners = {}
25: 
26:     class << self
27:       # Adds a listener class.
28:       # Automatically called when a class inherits from Redmine::Hook::Listener.
29:       def add_listener(klass)
30:         unless klass.include?(Singleton)
31:           raise "Hooks must include Singleton module."
32:         end
33: 
34:         @@listener_classes << klass
35:         clear_listeners_instances
36:       end
37: 
38:       # Returns all the listener instances.
39:       def listeners
40:         @@listeners ||= @@listener_classes.collect {|listener| listener.instance}
41:       end
42: 
43:       # Returns the listener instances for the given hook.
44:       def hook_listeners(hook)
45:         @@hook_listeners[hook] ||= listeners.select {|listener| listener.respond_to?(hook)}
46:       end
47: 
48:       # Clears all the listeners.
49:       def clear_listeners
50:         @@listener_classes = []
51:         clear_listeners_instances
52:       end
53: 
54:       # Clears all the listeners instances.
55:       def clear_listeners_instances
56:         @@listeners = nil
57:         @@hook_listeners = {}
58:       end
59: 
60:       # Calls a hook.
61:       # Returns the listeners response.
62:       def call_hook(hook, context={})
63:         [].tap do |response|
64:           hls = hook_listeners(hook)
65:           if hls.any?
66:             hls.each {|listener| response << listener.send(hook, context)}
67:           end
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     # Helper module included in ApplicationHelper and ActionController so that
73:     # hooks can be called in views like this:
74:     #
75:     #   <%= call_hook(:some_hook) %>
76:     #   <%= call_hook(:another_hook, :foo => 'bar') %>
77:     #
78:     # Or in controllers like:
79:     #   call_hook(:some_hook)
80:     #   call_hook(:another_hook, :foo => 'bar')
81:     #
82:     # Hooks added to views will be concatenated into a string. Hooks added to
83:     # controllers will return an array of results.
84:     #
85:     # Several objects are automatically added to the call context:
86:     #
87:     # * project => current project
88:     # * request => Request instance
89:     # * controller => current Controller instance
90:     # * hook_caller => object that called the hook
91:     #
92:     module Helper
93:       def call_hook(hook, context={})
94:         if is_a?(ActionController::Base)
95:           default_context = {:controller => self, :project => @project, :request => request, :hook_caller => self}
96:           Redmine::Hook.call_hook(hook, default_context.merge(context))
97:         else
98:           default_context = {:project => @project, :hook_caller => self}
99:           default_context[:controller] = controller if respond_to?(:controller)
100:           default_context[:request] = request if respond_to?(:request)
101:           Redmine::Hook.call_hook(hook, default_context.merge(context)).join(' ').html_safe
102:         end
103:       end
104:     end
105:   end
106: end
